headline: Dell leads broader AI hardware selloff as shares tumble 14%
summary: The selloff extends well beyond Dell. Hewlett Packard Enterprise is sliding roughly 8% and Super Micro Computer is off around 5%, confirming the move is concentrated in high-beta AI hardware names rather than broad technology. The Nasdaq-100 ETF (QQQ) is down about 1% on the session.

raw.headline: Dell leads broader AI hardware selloff as shares tumble 14%

headline: Two AI Server Bets, Two Outcomes: Dell Technologies vs Super Micro Computer
summary: Dell and Supermicro both rode the AI server wave this quarter, but one company came out looking like a disciplined giant while the other fumbled a story that should have been a slam dunk.

raw.headline: Two AI Server Bets, Two Outcomes: Dell Technologies vs Super Micro Computer

headline: Dell Falls 14%, HPE and Super Micro Slide as AI Hardware Stocks Give Back Gains
summary: AI hardware stocks are getting hit hard today, but the selloff may say more about crowded positioning than any crack in the underlying trade. Here is what is driving the moves in Dell, HPE, and Super Micro Computer and what investors should watch into the close.

raw.headline: Dell Falls 14%, HPE and Super Micro Slide as AI Hardware Stocks Give Back Gains

headline: 3 Reasons Investors Love Super Micro (SMCI)
summary: Over the last six months, Super Micro’s shares have sunk to $27.77, producing a disappointing 5.6% loss - a stark contrast to the S&P 500’s 8.2% gain. This might have investors contemplating their next move.

headline: Supermicro Expands End-to-End DCBBS Liquid Cooling Portfolio with Rear Door Heat Exchangers for High-Density AI and HPC Infrastructure
summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. (NASDAQ: SMCI), an AI, Enterprise, Storage, and 5G/Edge IT Total Solution Provider, featuring Data Center Building Block Solutions® (DCBBS), today announced the expansion of its Rear Door Heat Exchanger (RDHx) portfolio, further strengthening its end-to-end liquid cooling solutions for high-density AI and HPC infrastructure. headline: Corvex Appoints AI Infrastructure Veteran Michael Craig as Vice President of Architecture and Site Operations
summary: Corvex, Inc. (Nasdaq: MOVE), an engineering-led AI computing platform specializing in GPU-accelerated infrastructure for AI workloads, today announced the appointment of Michael Craig as Vice President of Architecture and Site Operations.
